title: Why Your RAG Pipeline Retrieves Garbage
hook: Your RAG retrieves garbage. Here's why.
takeaway: Chunk boundaries decide retrieval quality, not model size.
caption:
Your RAG retrieves garbage. Here's why.
Bad chunking breaks context before the embedding model ever sees it.
Save this before your next RAG debug session.
Follow @byteflowlabs for AI systems, no hype.

title: Why Longer Context Windows Don't Fix Bad Prompts
takeaway: Context rot kills accuracy long before the token limit does.
caption:
More context tokens != better answers.
Context rot buries your instructions in the middle of a 100k-token prompt.
Save this before you dump your whole codebase into one prompt.
Follow @byteflowlabs for AI systems, no hype.

title: Cosine Similarity Isn't Semantic Understanding
takeaway: Similarity scores measure geometry, not meaning.
caption:
Embeddings don't understand your query.
Cosine similarity just measures the angle between vectors, not intent.
Save this before trusting a top-1 embedding match blindly.
Follow @byteflowlabs for AI systems, no hype.

title: KV Cache Is Why Your LLM Slows Down Mid-Reply
hook: Your LLM gets slower the longer it talks.
takeaway: KV cache grows with every token, and so does latency.
caption:
Your LLM gets slower the longer it talks.
Every generated token grows the KV cache the model has to attend over.
Save this before you blame the model for a serving problem.
Follow @byteflowlabs for AI systems, no hype.

title: LLM-as-Judge Is Grading Its Own Homework
hook: Your eval LLM might be lying to you.
takeaway: A biased judge model gives you a biased benchmark.
caption:
Your eval LLM might be lying to you.
LLM-as-judge tends to favor its own family's writing style, not quality.
Save this before you trust a single-model eval score.
Follow @byteflowlabs for AI systems, no hype.

title: Model Routing Beats Always Using Flagship Models
hook: Stop paying flagship prices for easy questions.
takeaway: Route by difficulty, not habit, and costs drop fast.
caption:
Stop paying flagship prices for easy questions.
A router sends simple queries to a cheap model and hard ones to a strong one.
Save this before your token bill embarrasses you.
Follow @byteflowlabs for AI systems, no hype.

title: Prompt Injection Hides Inside 'Harmless' Text
hook: That PDF you fed your agent might attack it.
takeaway: Untrusted input is still input, even inside a tool result.
caption:
That PDF you fed your agent might attack it.
Prompt injection hides instructions inside documents, emails, and tool output.
Save this before you let an agent read untrusted content unchecked.
Follow @byteflowlabs for AI systems, no hype.

title: MCP Turns Every API Into a Tool an LLM Can Call
hook: Your LLM can't use tools it can't describe.
takeaway: A clean schema matters more than a clever prompt.
caption:
Your LLM can't use tools it can't describe.
MCP standardizes how a model discovers, calls, and gets results from external tools.
Save this before you hardcode another one-off tool integration.
